behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the CLI’s interaction with real git rename detection and subprocess output format. A unit/integration fake can validate parsing logic, but can miss real `git` rename semantics and status encoding. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it is about git diff categorization, not AI/ML decisions or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert report shows one rename and zero paired add+delete for that file.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Real failure often comes from subprocess byte output/decoding and real git binary diff behavior. Mocked diff text can hide encoding and process-edge failures. Higher levels are disqualified: no model output quality or multi-step agent pipeline is involved.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, add and modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI as a subprocess, assert exit code is success and binary file is counted in report.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The bug boundary is real git behavior on unborn HEAD (exit codes/messages), which is subprocess/OS-facing. In-process fakes may not reproduce exact error modes. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: no AI dependency or chained agentic steps.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, execute CLI subprocess, assert it exits successfully and prints an empty structured report.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This validates end-to-end correctness against actual git history, requiring real repository state transitions and git command behavior. Module-only tests can miss discrepancies introduced by real diff/history queries. Higher levels are disqualified: behavior does not depend on AI/ML output or agent pipelines.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits, run CLI subprocess for the target range, and assert category counts equal the known ground truth.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged correctness depends on real git index/worktree semantics and subprocess queries. Fakes of status output can miss subtle index interactions. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: deterministic git state comparison, not LLM behavior or multi-agent orchestration.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create modifications and capture CLI output in unstaged state; then stage the same changes and rerun; assert normalized structured output is identical.
